detail-block-widget
description: Detail screen → replace hand-drawn Unicode box borders with ratatui Block widget + padding. Add tui-scrollview for overflow when card content exceeds viewport.
notes:
## Feasibility
- Files: screens/detail.rs (primary — render_card() lines ~198-246), Cargo.toml (tui-scrollview dep)
- All other screens already use Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL) — detail.rs is sole outlier
- Block replacement is trivial (~50 lines, one function)
- tui-scrollview adds: new dep, ScrollViewState plumbing, scroll key events in detail screen
- Complexity: small (Block swap) to small-medium (with tui-scrollview)

title: syntect-code-highlighting
description: Syntax highlighting with syntect for code blocks in stream view. Highlight based on language tag from pulldown-cmark. Falls back to plain for unknown languages. Alternative: tui-markdown crate to replace custom markdown.rs entirely.
notes:
## Feasibility
- Files: markdown.rs (primary — code block rendering), nexus-tui/Cargo.toml (syntect dep), app.rs (CodeBlockRange)
- markdown.rs already uses pulldown-cmark but DISCARDS language tag — CodeBlock(_) doesn't bind CodeBlockKind
- Fix: change CodeBlock(_) to CodeBlock(ref kind), match CodeBlockKind::Fenced(lang)
- CAUTION: syntect has heavy defaults (oniguruma, large syntax/theme sets) — ~2MB binary impact
- tui-markdown alternative loses custom table rendering, word-wrap, CodeBlockRange detection
- Complexity: medium (syntect setup, Style RGB→Color::Rgb mapping, compile-time cost)

global-layout-polish
description: Global layout polish — padding, rounded borders, Tabs widget. Add Block::padding across all panels. BorderType::Rounded where appropriate. Tabs widget for screen nav. Paragraph::wrap in markdown.rs. Rect::centered() for overlay positioning.
notes:
## Feasibility
- Files: all 5 screens (dashboard, detail, health, projects, stream), markdown.rs, palette.rs
- Tab-cycling already implemented via KeyCode::Tab/BackTab — just need Tabs widget for visual
- Rect::centered() available since ratatui 0.26 (already in 0.29)
- Overlay centering currently manual arithmetic (area.width * 60 / 100) in 2+ places
- Paragraph::wrap used inconsistently — markdown.rs does manual table width capping
- No state/logic changes, purely rendering
- Complexity: small — cosmetic changes across ~6 files
close_reason: Wave 2: Table+ListState dashboard, Tabs nav, global rounded borders

ratatui-030-upgrade
description: Upgrade ratatui 0.29→0.30 — use ratatui::run(), Rect::centered() for overlays, block border merging, modular workspace. crossterm 0.28→0.29 compat. Foundation for all other UI work.
notes:
## Feasibility
- Files: Cargo.toml (workspace), main.rs (terminal init/teardown), palette.rs/projects.rs/detail.rs/stream.rs (overlay Rect math)
- ratatui::run() replaces manual crossterm boilerplate (~50 LOC rewrite in main.rs)
- 4 overlay sites use manual Rect math → Rect::centered()
- crossterm 0.28→0.29 has minor API changes (MouseEventKind, KeyEvent field renames)
- No active specs, no conflicts
- Complexity: small-medium

tui-textarea-input
description: Input bar → tui-textarea widget. Replace manual input rendering in stream.rs and scratchpad editor in projects.rs. Gets cursor positioning, selection, copy/paste, undo for free.
notes:
## Feasibility
- Files: screens/stream.rs (render_input_bar), screens/projects.rs (render_scratchpad), app.rs (stream_input/scratchpad_text fields), Cargo.toml
- ISSUE: render functions take &App (read-only) but TextArea owns mutable state — needs borrow plumbing change
- Must preserve executing-spinner branch in stream input bar
- Both current widgets are hand-rolled (string + block cursor char)
- tui-textarea key events go through .input() method — need to reconcile with app-level history nav
- Complexity: small-medium (render swap trivial, but lifetime/ownership + borrow plumbing adds work)

health-gauge-sparkline
description: Health view → LineGauge for CPU/RAM, Sparkline for activity history, rounded card borders, Docker container table. Replace text percentages with visual meters.
notes:
## Feasibility
- Files: screens/health.rs (primary), nexus-core/src/health.rs (MachineHealth struct), app.rs (history buffer)
- BLOCKER: No historical data collected — MachineHealth is single snapshot. Sparkline needs Vec<u64> history buffer
- Requires: new history field in core types, ring-buffer accumulation in TUI client, possibly API version bump
- LineGauge + rounded Block borders are straightforward widget swaps
- cpu_color() thresholds already exist — can drive gauge_style
- Complexity: medium (visual changes easy, Sparkline data model is non-trivial)

[SPEC] status-bar-telemetry
description: Rate limit, cost, model telemetry via gRPC for remote TUI status bars

title: status bar telemetry via grpc for remote clients
description: Send all telemetry needed to emulate the TUI status bar (agent dots, session count, uptime) via gRPC so remote clients (Mac TUI, future web) can render equivalent status bars
notes:
## Feasibility
- All data already exists in proto (HealthResponse, SessionList)
- Need GetHealth RPC or extend SessionList with uptime/connection state
- TUI uptime currently computed client-side — remote needs agent-reported uptime
- Complexity: small — wiring existing data, no new types
- Touches: proto/nexus.proto, grpc.rs, api.rs, dashboard.rs, stream.rs
## Account Telemetry
- CC emits rate_limit_event in stream-json: utilization %, rateLimitType (seven_day), surpassedThreshold, isUsingOverage
- Parser should capture these and surface in status bar: usage gauge (91% of 7-day cap), warning threshold indicator
- Also capture from result events: total_cost_usd, model, duration_api_ms, service_tier
- Could show: model name, cost-per-command, rate limit gauge alongside existing infra metrics

title: improve tui aesthetics like opencode or claude code
description: Improve the nexus TUI UI to feel more like OpenCode or Claude Code. Better layout, smoother transitions, richer formatting, syntax highlighting in output, better use of borders and spacing.
notes:
## Scope
- Study OpenCode TUI (github.com/nicholasgasior/opencode) for layout patterns
- Study Claude Code's terminal UI for formatting patterns
- Better markdown rendering in stream view output
- Syntax highlighting for code blocks
- Smoother screen transitions
- Richer status indicators and progress display
- Complexity: medium — UI polish across multiple screens

project-level badges and scratchpad in tui
description: Surface badges at a project level in the TUI, and include a scratch pad for ideas
notes:
## Feasibility
- Touches: nexus-core/src/session.rs, nexus-tui/src/screens/projects.rs, nexus-tui/src/app.rs, new nexus-core/src/project.rs
- Prior art: projects.rs already renders 7-col table (badge col follows same Row pattern); InputMode enum supports new ScratchpadEdit mode; color tokens exist
- Blockers: No project-level metadata persistence yet; agent doesn't read .beads/; scratchpad needs tui-textarea or hand-rolled widget; detail-palette-start spec could conflict in app.rs
- Complexity: medium — badges need agent to read .beads/ and expose via gRPC; scratchpad needs new input mode + text editor widget

deploy monitoring and machine sync per project
description: Monitor latest deploy and send via telemetry per project. Projects can be triggered from any machine — monitor how out of sync a machine is. Maybe have a fast-forward button. Build out project registry per machine.
notes:
## Scope
- Track last deploy commit/timestamp per project per machine
- Show sync status: how many commits behind is each machine
- Fast-forward button: trigger git pull + rebuild on target machine
- Per-machine project registry (which projects exist where)
- Touches: proto (ProjectSync message), agent (git status tracking), TUI (sync indicator)
- Complexity: medium — needs cross-machine coordination

title: project list from .claude/projects for new session wizard
description: nexus-agent should scan ~/.claude/projects/ to build a project list. TUI's [n] new session wizard should show a selectable list instead of free-text project input.
notes:
## Feasibility
- Agent scans ~/.claude/projects/ dirs (trivial fs::read_dir)
- New ListProjects RPC in proto (one message + one method)
- TUI already has list-select widget pattern (agent-select step)
- Swap render_text_prompt for render_project_select in StartSessionProject
- Remote agents list their own projects, not TUI machine's
- Complexity: small — reuse agent-select pattern for project selection
- Touches: nexus.proto, grpc.rs, client.rs, app.rs, palette.rs
